client.role: Defendant in Musk v. Altman; CEO of OpenAI
client.notes: Defending Altman's position in Musk v. Altman.

allegations.summary: Altman engaged in a 'long con' to convert a 501(c)(3) into a for-profit enterprise in violation of the founding agreement.
Altman induced contributions of capital and reputation under false pretenses regarding the nonprofit mission.
Altman should disgorge approximately $134B in gains derived from the for-profit conversion.
The press release on the conversion contradicted internal filings.

evidence.summary: Internal communications from 2017 show plaintiff was a participant in early for-profit conversion discussions.
The conversion was approved by the board with full disclosure of structure and economic terms.
Plaintiff founded a competing for-profit AI company (xAI) shortly before filing this action.
Compute requirements for frontier models exceeded what nonprofit funding could realistically provide.
